authorization: Authorize trading research session synthesis and next-direction memo only.

DR10_TURNOVER_RISK.corroborated_at_t1_audit: This session's read-only audit at 54873eb CORROBORATED (HIGH) the parallel verdict
DR10_TURNOVER_RISK.defined_as: turnover_cost_explosion DR-rule. v1: annual_turnover>0.50 OR S2_cost_drag>0.05. v2 (s14+ only): annual_turnover>0.50 AND S2_cost_drag>0.05.
DR10_TURNOVER_RISK.exposed_at_s13_d1: P6.5 cost-stress matrix fired DR10 v1 on annual_turnover branch (84.7851 vs 0.50; 169.6x over) while S2_cost_drag branch did NOT fire (2.35% vs 5.00%)
DR10_TURNOVER_RISK.status_under_v1: REJECT_FAST verdict on s13-d1 stands verbatim
DR10_TURNOVER_RISK.status_under_v2: For NEW s14+ candidates only: same metrics would NOT fire DR10 (AND conjunction requires both branches; S2_cost_drag still does NOT fire). This is forward-binding only; does NOT change s13-d1 verdict.

K9_DR10_STRUCTURAL_TENSION.arithmetic_at_retail_scale: At $200k equity with 0.5pct per-trade risk and MNQ.c.0 per-trade notional ~$75k, max DR10-clearing trades-per-year is ~1.33. K9 requires >=22/y. Gap: 16-17x apart.
K9_DR10_STRUCTURAL_TENSION.defined_as: For single-instrument per-trade-sized strategies on multi-year windows, K9 and DR10 v1 thresholds form a structural tension. K9 demands MORE trades (>=22/y for 4.6y window); DR10 v1 demands LESS turnover (annual_turnover<0.50).
K9_DR10_STRUCTURAL_TENSION.framework_response: Parallel session investigation plan 28cbaea formalized this incompatibility; v2 SEAL at 78cd22e changed the rule from OR to AND, partially relieving tension for new candidates by requiring BOTH branches.
K9_DR10_STRUCTURAL_TENSION.mathematical_consequence: For single futures instrument at retail scale, K9 and DR10 v1 are not jointly satisfiable under conventional sizing. Either lower notional drastically, or raise DR10 threshold, or pivot universe.

K9_REACHABILITY.carried_forward_to_t1_and_s13_d1: T1 + s13-d1 explicitly carried K9-reachability discipline in PLAN/DRAFT/SEAL; conjectured 46-68 IS trades/year (210-313 over 4.6y); actual 159 over 4.6y (=34.4/y) BELOW lower bound but ABOVE absolute K9 threshold
K9_REACHABILITY.defined_as: At PLAN/SEAL turn, candidate must analyze IS+OOS trade-density and demonstrate K9 closed_trades threshold (>=100 per window) is plausibly reachable given mechanic-family signal density on chosen universe.
K9_REACHABILITY.falsified_at_s12_d1: s12-D1 PLAN conjectured 80-200 IS trades; P6 IS produced 33-48 trades. Hypothesis falsified at IS.
K9_REACHABILITY.origin: S10-D2 P10 OOS K9 sub-threshold finding triggered the introduction of explicit K9-reachability discipline at s12-D1 PLAN.

WHY_DONCHIAN_VARIANTS_FAILED_TRADE_DENSI.conclusion: F1 family is signal-density-limited on liquid futures at the K9=100 threshold. To clear K9, F1 must be paired with high-frequency universes or relaxed cap rules - both of which open separate risks.
WHY_DONCHIAN_VARIANTS_FAILED_TRADE_DENSI.mechanic_property: Donchian breakout requires extended price-range expansion to trigger entries; on liquid index futures this is structurally infrequent. Reparam-cap (no pyramid) further reduces re-entry opportunities.
WHY_DONCHIAN_VARIANTS_FAILED_TRADE_DENSI.s10_d2_evidence: S10-D2 cross-asset Donchian no-pyramid reparam-cap on NQ+GC+ZN+CL produced 53 OOS trades over its window; K9 OOS gating triggered sub-threshold disposition.
WHY_DONCHIAN_VARIANTS_FAILED_TRADE_DENSI.s12_d1_evidence: S12-D1 single-instrument Donchian-15/8 on MNQ.c.0 produced 33-48 IS trades over 4.6297y window; conjectured 80-200; falsified at IS.
WHY_DONCHIAN_VARIANTS_FAILED_TRADE_DENSI.summary: F1 Donchian trend on liquid futures (cross-asset basket at s10-D2; single-instrument MNQ.c.0 at s12-D1) does not produce sufficient signal density to clear K9.

WHY_RSI_2_SOLVED_TRADE_DENSITY_BUT_FAILE.arithmetic_of_the_swap: F1 Donchian-15/8 ~7-10 trades/year on MNQ.c.0; below K9. F3 RSI(2) ~34 trades/year on MNQ.c.0; clears K9 but blows past DR10 v1.
WHY_RSI_2_SOLVED_TRADE_DENSITY_BUT_FAILE.dr10_failed_v1: Annual_turnover 84.7851 vs DR10 v1 threshold 0.50 = 169.6x over. REJECT_FAST under v1.
WHY_RSI_2_SOLVED_TRADE_DENSITY_BUT_FAILE.dr10_v2_relief_partial: Under v2 (s14+ only): same s13-d1 metrics would NOT fire DR10 because v2 requires BOTH branches (annual_turnover AND S2_cost_drag) - the S2_cost_drag branch (2.35%) is still below threshold (5.00%). This means under v2, an RSI(2)-like signal density would clear DR10. But this is forward-binding only; s13-d1 verdict stands at REJECT_FAST under v1.
WHY_RSI_2_SOLVED_TRADE_DENSITY_BUT_FAILE.k9_solved: Parallel s13-d1 P6 IS produced 159 closed_trades over 4.6297y = 34.4 trades/year. K9 threshold 100 cleared with 1.59x margin.
WHY_RSI_2_SOLVED_TRADE_DENSITY_BUT_FAILE.load_bearing_lesson: Solving K9 by increasing signal density typically pushes turnover ratio above DR10 v1 threshold for single-instrument per-trade-sized strategies. The 22-vs-1.33 trades-per-year structural gap means most reasonable mechanic families on single futures at retail scale will land in one of two failure modes under v1: K9 fail (too few trades) OR DR10 fail (too much turnover).
WHY_RSI_2_SOLVED_TRADE_DENSITY_BUT_FAILE.summary: F3 RSI(2) bi-directional mean-reversion solved K9 by increasing signal density 4-5x over F1 Donchian, but the same higher density drove annual_turnover 169.6x over DR10 v1 threshold.
